perturbation.original_text: 1 CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ("Agreement ") between HERNIC FERROCHROME PRORPIETARY LIMITED (IN BUSINESS RESCUE) ("Disclosing Party ") and THE RECEIVING PARTY IDENTIFIED IN ANNEXURE A ("Receiving Party”) (collectively, " Parties " and a reference to " Party " shall be a reference to either of them as the context requires) 1 INTERPRETATION
perturbation.changed_text: 1 CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT ("Agreement ") between HERNIC FERROCHROME PRORPIETARY LIMITED (IN BUSINESS RESCUE) ("Disclosing Party ") and THE EVALUATING ENTITY IDENTIFIED IN ANNEXURE A ("Receiving Party”) (collectively, " Parties " and a reference to " Party " shall be a reference to either of them as the context requires) 1 INTERPRETATION
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Evaluating Entity' in the initial definition introduces confusion, as all subsequent references to 'Receiving Party' within the agreement are not explicitly redefined, this leads to an in-text contradiction within the document, especially in sections outlining their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the unauthorised use or disclosure of any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party may give rise to substantial damage to the Disclosing Party . Accordingly , the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party that, for as long as this Agreement remains in force, it will (and it will procure that its employees, officers, directors, representatives , advise rs and any Affiliate to which it discloses the Confidential Information will) – 4.2.1 treat as confidential all Confidential Information; 4.2.2 protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in the same manner and with the same degree of care that a reasonable man would use to protect his own confidential information; 4.2.3 not use, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever apply or reproduce the Confidential Information except to the extent required to achieve the Purpose; 4.2.4 not divulge or permit access to the Confiden tial Information to any person except to those of its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and Affiliates who have a specific need to know such information in relation to the Transaction . Before revealing any Confidential Information to its employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates , the Receiving Party shall be obliged to notify such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates of the confidential nature of t he disclosure and ensure that such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates are obliged not to disclose the Confidential Information to any unauthorised third party and not use the Confidential Information except as may b e strictly required in relation to the Transaction . The Receiving Party shall be liable for any damages incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any unauthorised disclosure of the Confidential Information made by the Receiving Party, it s employees, officers, directors, representatives and /or Affiliates; and 4.2.5 immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing if it becomes aware that there has been an unauthorised disclosure or use of any Confidential Information and take all necessary steps to recov er such Confidential Information and to prevent any further disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Evaluating Entity' in the definition, while retaining 'Receiving Party' as the defined term, creates an in-text contradiction. Section 4.2 uses 'Receiving Party' and outlines their obligations regarding confidential information. Since the agreement now defines 'Receiving Party' as 'Evaluating Entity' but continues to use 'Receiving Party', it is unclear who is bound by the obligations in 4.2, leading to confusion and potential unenforceability. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as per the provided definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Parties intend to enter into discussions regarding the proposed disposal by the Disclosing Party of its business as a going concern , or such other interest as may be provided for in the process documentation which may, subject to 2.4 and 2.5, be provided to the Receiving Party , ("Tran saction " or "Purpose ").
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Parties intend to enter into discussions regarding the proposed disposal by the Disclosing Party of its business as a going concern , or such other interest as may be provided for in the process documentation which may, subject to 2.4 and 2.5, be provided to the Prospecting Entity , ("Tran saction " or "Purpose ").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Prospecting Entity' introduces ambiguity. Later sections still refer to 'Receiving Party,' creating confusion about which entity the obligations apply to. This introduces an in-text contradiction affecting clauses related to obligations and liabilities of the 'Receiving Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 The Disclosing Party may, at any time, request the Receiving Party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party , its Affiliates or any of their respective employees, representatives, of ficers, directors or advis ers, in relation to the Transaction and may, in addition, request the Receiving Party to furnish a written statement sworn under oath to the effect that, upon such return, neither the Receiving Party nor any of its Affiliates have retained in their possession, or under their control, either directly or indirectly, any such material or any copies thereof or extracts therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Prospecting Entity' in Section 2.1 creates a direct contradiction with Section 6.1, which refers to the 'Receiving Party'. Section 6.1 outlines obligations of the 'Receiving Party' regarding confidential information. Since the text now uses two different terms for the same party, creating ambiguity about which entity Section 6.1 applies to, this creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability of those obligations, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 For purposes of this Agreement, " Confidential Information " shall include, without limitation, any information or other data relating to the Disclosing Party or any of its Affiliates, which is disclosed to the Receiving Party , whether prior to the conclusion of this Agreement, in terms of this Agreement or otherwise in connection with this Agreement or the Transaction (including but not limited to the Transaction itself and any negotiations in relation to the Transaction ), includ ing this Agreement, its contents and the negotiations leading up to the conclusion of this Agreement -
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 For purposes of this Agreement, " Confidential Information " shall include, without limitation, any information or other data relating to the Disclosing Party or any of its Affiliates, which is disclosed to the Interested Organization , whether prior to the conclusion of this Agreement, in terms of this Agreement or otherwise in connection with this Agreement or the Transaction (including but not limited to the Transaction itself and any negotiations in relation to the Transaction ), includ ing this Agreement, its contents and the negotiations leading up to the conclusion of this Agreement -
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'Receiving Party' with 'Interested Organization' in the definition of Confidential Information generates ambiguity. Subsequent clauses outlining the 'Receiving Party's' responsibilities concerning Confidential Information lack clarity, potentially weakening enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.5 Should the Receiving Party or any of its employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers or Affiliates be obliged by any law, or a valid orde r of any court of competent jurisdiction or government authority acting with the powers granted to it in law, to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party, the Receiving Party shall, prio r to making any such disclosure – 4.5.1 immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such request, so that the Disclosing Party may seek an interdict or other appropriate remedy to limit the s cope of any such disclosure; 4.5.2 consult with the Disclosing Party on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and co -operate with the Disclosing Party in taking such steps as the Disclosing Party may reasonably require to res ist or narrow any su ch request; and 4.5.3 if disclosure of any Confidential Information is legally required, exercise all reasonable endeavours to obtain an order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be a fforded to such portion of the Confidential Informati on as the Disclosing Party may designate.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as information disclosed to the 'Receiving Party'. The changed text alters this to 'Interested Organization'. This creates a contradiction because section 4.5 refers to the 'Receiving Party' and their obligations regarding confidential information. By changing the definition of who receives the confidential information, it becomes unclear whether the obligations outlined in 4.5 apply to the 'Interested Organization', thus weakening enforcement and creating ambiguity. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Disclosing Party is under no obligation to disclo se any Confidential Information or any other information or documentation relating to the Transaction to the Receiving Party, notwithstanding the conclusion of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 The Potential Acquirer acknowledges that the Disclosing Party is under no obligation to disclo se any Confidential Information or any other information or documentation relating to the Transaction to the Receiving Party, notwithstanding the conclusion of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Receiving Party' with 'Potential Acquirer' in this clause creates a contradiction. The sentence continues to refer to the 'Receiving Party' later in the clause, implying the Disclosing Party has no obligation to the 'Potential Acquirer' to disclose information, but an obligation to the Receiving Party, but now the receiving party is undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 The Potential Acquirer acknowledges that the Disclosing Party is under no obligation to disclo se any Confidential Information or any other information or documentation relating to the Transaction to the Receiving Party, notwithstanding the conclusion of this Agreement. The Receiving Party further acknowledges that, a s a condition to receiving a ny documentation and/or information relating to the Transaction , the Receiving Party is required to conclude this Agreement and bind itself to the undertakings herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Receiving Party'. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Potential Acquirer' in the first instance but retains 'Receiving Party' later in the same clause. The contradicted text has both 'Potential Acquirer' and 'Receiving Party', leading to a contradiction because it is unclear which party the obligation applies to after the replacement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the change creates conflicting obligations based on terminology.

perturbation.original_text: 3 TERM OF AGREEMENT This Agreement will commence on the Signature D ate and shall remain binding and in force for a period of five years thereafter or such longer period as the Parties may agree to in writing ("Term ").
perturbation.changed_text: 3 TERM OF AGREEMENT This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and shall remain binding and in force for a period of five years thereafter or such longer period as the Parties may agree to in writing ("Term ").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Signature Date' to 'Effective Date' without clarifying if they are the same creates ambiguity. Later references to 'Signature Date' now conflict with the initial term definition, affecting clauses relying on the 'Signature Date' for timelines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 who is, as at the Signature Date or during the course of the Parties discussions relating to the Pu rpose, in the employ of the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates ; and
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Signature Date' to 'Effective Date' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Signature Date'. If the agreement commences on the 'Effective Date' as changed, any clause relying on 'Signature Date' for timelines or eligibility, like clause 7.1, becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, as it's unclear whether 'Signature Date' and 'Effective Date' are the same. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the contract now uses two terms ('Signature Date' and 'Effective Date') interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the term " Affiliate " shall mean any person which either directly or indirectly controls, is directly or indirectly controlled by, 3 or is under common control with a Party and, for purposes of t his definition, a person shall be deemed to "control" an entity if it owns or has the ability to vote, directly or indirectly, more than 50% of the voting securities or equivalent interest of that entity and a reference to an Affiliate shall include a refe rence to such Affiliate's employees, officers, directors , representatives and advis ers.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the term " Associate " shall mean any person which either directly or indirectly controls, is directly or indirectly controlled by, 3 or is under common control with a Party and, for purposes of t his definition, a person shall be deemed to "control" an entity if it owns or has the ability to vote, directly or indirectly, more than 50% of the voting securities or equivalent interest of that entity and a reference to an Affiliate shall include a refe rence to such Affiliate's employees, officers, directors , representatives and advis ers.
perturbation.explanation: Defining 'Affiliate' as 'Associate' in section 4.1 while still referencing 'Affiliate' later introduces ambiguity, particularly affecting the scope of non-solicitation and confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 The Receiving Party acknowledges that the unauthorised use or disclosure of any Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party may give rise to substantial damage to the Disclosing Party . Accordingly , the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party that, for as long as this Agreement remains in force, it will (and it will procure that its employees, officers, directors, representatives , advise rs and any Affiliate to which it discloses the Confidential Information will) – 4.2.1 treat as confidential all Confidential Information; 4.2.2 protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in the same manner and with the same degree of care that a reasonable man would use to protect his own confidential information; 4.2.3 not use, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever apply or reproduce the Confidential Information except to the extent required to achieve the Purpose; 4.2.4 not divulge or permit access to the Confiden tial Information to any person except to those of its employees , officers, directors , representatives , advisers and Affiliates who have a specific need to know such information in relation to the Transaction . Before revealing any Confidential Information to its employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates , the Receiving Party shall be obliged to notify such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates of the confidential nature of t he disclosure and ensure that such employees , officers, directors, representatives , advisers and/or Affiliates are obliged not to disclose the Confidential Information to any unauthorised third party and not use the Confidential Information except as may b e strictly required in relation to the Transaction . The Receiving Party shall be liable for any damages incurred by the Disclosing Party as a result of any unauthorised disclosure of the Confidential Information made by the Receiving Party, it s employees, officers, directors, representatives and /or Affiliates; and 4.2.5 immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing if it becomes aware that there has been an unauthorised disclosure or use of any Confidential Information and take all necessary steps to recov er such Confidential Information and to prevent any further disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Affiliate,' which is used in section 4.2 regarding confidential information. The changed text replaces 'Affiliate' with 'Associate' in the definition in section 4.1, while section 4.2 still refers to 'Affiliates.' This creates a contradiction because the contract now references a term ('Affiliate') that is not clearly defined, potentially altering the scope of confidentiality obligations outlined in section 4.2. Specifically, it is unclear whether the confidentiality obligations now extend to 'Associates' instead of 'Affiliates' or if the term 'Affiliates' in 4.2 is now undefined, creating ambiguity as defined in 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
